findings.code_snippet:
for key, value in config.items():
    os.environ[key] = value
findings.recommendation: Avoid side-effects in configuration modules. Apply config only when explicitly requested or in a less intrusive lifecycle hook.
findings.reference: Configuration Management

findings.description: Imports inside functions create overhead and potential import loops or hidden dependencies.
findings.recommendation: Move imports to the top of the file for better performance and dependency visibility.
findings.reference: Code Quality

findings.description: Console logs contain emojis which can break log parsing and grep commands.
findings.recommendation: Use standard logging module with levels instead of print statements with emojis.
findings.reference: Error Handling

findings.description: Hardcoded model endpoints and ports should use environment variables for flexibility and security
findings.recommendation: Use environment variables: TENSORRT_EMBEDDING_ENDPOINT = os.environ.get("TENSORRT_EMBEDDING_ENDPOINT", "http://localhost:8051")

findings.description: Hardcoded model names should be configurable via environment variables for maintainability
findings.recommendation: Use environment variable for model names to allow easy switching between deployments

findings.description: TRUST_REMOTE_CODE = True poses significant security risk as it allows loading untrusted code from models
findings.recommendation: Set to False by default, only enable with explicit user authorization and security scanning

findings.description: Unlimited timeout (0 seconds) for HTTP requests can cause system hangs and resource exhaustion
findings.recommendation: Set a reasonable timeout like 60s-300s; if truly multi-agent, use timeout manager with exponential backoff

findings.description: HTTP protocol used for model endpoints instead of HTTPS exposes model data to interception
findings.recommendation:
Use HTTPS for all external endpoints, even for localhost: TENSORRT_EMBEDDING_ENDPOINT: str = "https://localhost:8051"
      WARN_USE_HTTPS: bool = os.environ.get("USE_HTTPS", True)

findings.description: Magic numbers (500, 300, 120) lack clear documentation for their derivation
findings.code_snippet: DOCUMENT_PROCESSING_TIMEOUT: int = 500, EMBEDDING_TIMEOUT: int = 300, SEARCH_TIMEOUT: int = 120
findings.recommendation: Add constants with clear naming: MAX_PROCESSING_TIMEOUT = 500s (as variables/consts)

findings.description: Email/node affinity and reserved CPUs should be configurable via environment variables
findings.recommendation: Allow customization via environment: JINA_RAG_RESERVED_CPUS = int(os.environ.get("JINA_RAG_RESERVED_CPUS", "2"))

findings.description: Harcoaed character limits (1000000 chars) may need adjustment for different use cases
findings.recommendation: Use named constant constants like MAX_TOTAL_CHAR_LIMIT = 1_000_000 for clarity

findings.description: Large batch sizes without validation across different hardware could cause OOM errors
findings.recommendation: Implement dynamic batch sizing based on RAM/GPU available: batch_size = min(DEFAULT_BATCH_SIZE, AVAILABLE_MEMORY // MINIMAL_SIZE)

findings.description: Environment variables are being used to set default values which could be incomplete or inconsistent across environments. Consider using only environment variables or a dedicated settings module.
findings.code_snippet: LOG_LEVEL = 7  # 🔧 OTTIMIZZAZIONE: Livello ottimizzato (nasconde log DEBUG_FLOW verbosi)
findings.recommendation: Use environment variable: LOG_LEVEL = int(os.getenv('LOG_LEVEL', '10').strip()) or document why magic number 7 is used
findings.reference: Configuration Management

findings.description: Funzioni get_effective_default_llm() caricano e leggono custom_settings.json a ogni chiamata senza caching, generando redundant I/O operations
findings.recommendation: Implementezimi un caching mechanism per memorizzare i risultati di get_effective_default_llm() una volta calcolati
findings.reference: Anti-Patterns - Maintainability - Missing caching for expensive operations

findings.description: File di 600+ righe per un file di configurazione: presenterà difficoltà di manutenzione e debugging
findings.recommendation: Considera di dividere il file di configurazione in moduli separati per categoria (databse, LLM, API, feature flags)
findings.reference: Anti-Patterns - Maintainability - Dead code (unused functions/variables)

findings.description: Model names (vllm/qwen3-30b-a3b-thinking-2507-awq-4bit) ripetuti ~20 volte nel file senza centralizzazione
findings.recommendation: Crea config oggetti/constants per LLM models e usa le chiavi di progetto
findings.reference: Anti-Patterns - Maintainability - Magic numbers (unexplained constants)

findings.description: Multiple file I/O calls for custom_settings.json in three separate functions without shared state
findings.recommendation: Create a single ConfigSingleton or CachingManager for configuration loading
findings.reference: Anti-Patterns - Maintainability - Copy-paste programming

findings.description: DEFAULT_LLM_FALLBACK returns None which may cause NoneType errors downstream
findings.recommendation: Use 'or' or 'NOT' to ensure a valid string is always returned
findings.reference: Anti-Patterns - Maintainability - Dead code (unused functions/variables)

findings.description: Non-careful use of magic bytes for config (PDF_IMAGE_MIN_SIZE = 100)
findings.recommendation: Move to environment variables or at least better named constants
findings.reference: Anti-Patterns - Maintainability - Magic numbers (unexplained constants)

findings.description: Blocking I/O operations during startup. The module performs network requests (requests.get) and subprocess calls synchronously, blocking the main thread.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to async network clients (e.g., aiohttp or httpx) and use async/await to prevent blocking the event loop, especially in multi-threaded or async environments.

findings.description: Startup latency penalty. Configuration and network probing logic executes immediately on import, increasing load time before application does work.
findings.recommendation: Initialize configuration lazily or at application startup, not at module import time, to reduce startup overhead and prevent blocking other imports.

findings.description: Unnecessary file I/O overhead. Reading '/proc/version' and '/etc/resolv.conf' synchronously inside the WSL detection function on every import or config request.
findings.recommendation: Cache OS type detection results or defer file system checks. Avoid opening heavy OS files repeatedly if system state changes rarely.

findings.description: Redundant connection setup. Each call to test_connection creates a new connection without a Session object, missing connection pooling benefits.
findings.recommendation: Use requests.Session() to reuse TCP connections across retries in the loop, reducing network overhead when testing multiple hosts.

findings.description: Nome del file di configurazione hardcodato (models.json) in contrasto con la best practice 'NO hardcoded parameters in code'.
findings.recommendation: Sempre la variabile di ambiente 'MODELS_CONFIG_PATH' o usare un loader di configurazione esterno configurato all'avvio.

findings.description: Duplicazione del codice: la logica di ricerca nei file JSON (vllm_models e ollama_models) è presente sia in get_model_capabilities che in get_model_info.
findings.code_snippet:
for model in cls._models_data.get("vllm_models", []):
...for model in cls._models_data.get("ollama_models", []):
findings.recommendation: Creare un metodo privato '_find_model' che gestisca la ricerca, utilizzato sia da get_model_capabilities che da get_model_info.

findings.description: Eccezioni swallowed e logged tramite print() invece di un sistema di logging strutturato, rischiando perdite di contesto per debug.
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare il modulo 'logging' con livelli specifici (ERROR) e contesto aggiuntivo (timestamp, process ID).

findings.description: Import qui in disuso: 'import os' non è usato se项目的建设采用 Path (pathlib).
findings.recommendation: Rimuovere l'import inutile per pulire lo spazio dei nomi.

findings.description: Funzioni modulari 'helper' che duplicano metodi di classe esplici presenti poi nel class.
findings.recommendation: Riutilizzare direttamente i metodi della classe o deprecare le funzioni standalone per evitare confusione pubblica/privata.

findings.description: Logica di caricamento handlers (Refresh_cache) resettata毎回ではなく，dovrebbe essere una scelta esplicita o gestita tramite changement.
findings.recommendation: Evitare di manipolare direttamente lo stato interno `_initialized` o `_models_data` publicamente, usare metodi getter/setter invece.

findings.description: Default security setting 'TRUST_REMOTE_CODE' is set to True, which allows arbitrary code execution by models. This creates a security vulnerability by default.
findings.recommendation: Set to False as default. Configure this strictly via environment variables in production environments.

findings.description: Endpoints are hardcoded (localhost:8051, localhost:8050). This prevents the configuration from running in containerized or microservices environments where services run on public IPs or different ports.
findings.recommendation: Move endpoint configurations to a .env file or external config store. Use 'localhost' only for local development or controlled environment variables.

findings.description: Use of module-level global singleton 'RAG_CONFIG' encourages global state (Anti-Pattern). This makes unit testing difficult and couples modules tightly to this file.
findings.recommendation: Pass RAGConfig instance into modules via dependency injection. Remove module-level global instantiation.

findings.description: Configuration contains 'Magic Numbers' (e.g., batch size 4, 1000, 1000000) directly in code without clear constants or documentation explaining why these specific values were chosen.
findings.recommendation: Define clear constant names and documentation describing thresholds for these limits, or move them to a dedicated config file.

findings.description: Contains informal comments and excessive use of emojis in documentation code, which can reduce professionalism and readability for developers.
findings.recommendation: Remove emojis and maintain formal documentation style. Focus on what the setting does rather than technical humor unless it aids specific team understanding.

findings.description: Logging messages should include domain and level metadata for automated filtering
findings.code_snippet: logging.info(f"🔍 TRACE FILTERS: Configurati filtri globali - exclude={len(GLOBAL_TRACE_EXCLUDE)} pattern, include={len(GLOBAL_TRACE_INCLUDE)} pattern")
findings.recommendation: Use structured logging with consistent domain and level: logger={'domain': 'trace','level': 'info', 'message': 'TRACE FILTERS: Configurati filtri globali...'}
findings.reference: General Principles #4 Error Handling

findings.description: Logging messages should include domain and level metadata for automated filtering
findings.code_snippet: logging.warning(f"⚠️ TRACE FILTERS: Errore nella configurazione filtri globali: {e}")
findings.recommendation: Use structured logging with consistent domain and level: logger={'domain': 'trace','level': 'warning', 'message': 'TRACE FILTERS: Errore...'}
findings.reference: General Principles #4 Error Handling

findings.description: Hardcoded emoji and special characters in log strings make log parsing difficult
findings.code_snippet: logging.info(f"🔍 TRACE FILTERS: Configurati filtri globali...")
findings.recommendation: Remove emoji from log messages and use a consistent log design: logging.info('TRACE FILTERS')

findings.description: Exception handler catches all exceptions and swallows the original exception details
findings.recommendation: Re-raise the exception or log it properly without losing stack trace: raise e or re-raise exc_info=True in logging.exception()
findings.reference: General Principles #4 Error Handling - No silently swallow exceptions

findings.description: Exception handler should specify which exception type is caught rather than generic Exception
findings.recommendation: Be more specific about the exception you want to catch or log the generic Exception with context specified
findings.reference: General Principles #4 Error Handling

findings.description: Import statement inside function should be at module level for performance
findings.recommendation: Move the import statement to module level to avoid repeated imports on each function call
findings.reference: Best practices #4 Configuration Management - Configuration should be loaded at startup, not scattered throughout the code

findings.description: Magic string patterns '_*_' should be documented as they have special meaning in fnmatch
findings.recommendation: Add a comment explaining that wildcard pattern '_' matches any character set for security: this pattern is very permissive
findings.reference: General Principles #6 Code Quality - Magic numbers

findings.description: Copying lists creates unnecessary memory overhead on every function call
findings.code_snippet:
exclude=GLOBAL_TRACE_EXCLUDE.copy(),
include=GLOBAL_TRACE_INCLUDE.copy() if GLOBAL_TRACE_INCLUDE else None
findings.recommendation: Use lazy evaluation or pass references directly to reduce memory overhead, especially if these are large lists
findings.reference: Performance Anti-Patterns - Unnecessary memory operations

findings.recommendation: Aggiuna validazione dello schema del JSON custom_settings.json prima del parsing. Filtra input non attendibili per prevenire l'esecuzione di codice malizioso o la sovrascrittura di parametri critici di sicurezza (es. API keys o headers).
I nomi dei modelli LLM sono hardcoded e specifiche di implementazione (Code Agnosticism). Configura i nomi dei modelli tramite variabili d'ambiente o un file di configurazione esettérnamente.
Il default URL è hardcoded verso localhost. Evita di esporti parametri di rete sensibili o localhost direttamente nel codice. Usa variabili d'ambiente per la base URL del servizio TTS.
Leggere il file JSON a ogni chiamata computazionale (get_effective_default_llm) impatta le performance. Carica le impostazioni nel momento dell'avvio (.py) e usa un cache in memoria.
I valori per i log ( livello 7) sono hardcoded constants numeriche (Magic Numbers). Definisci questi parametri come costanti spiegabili o leggibili da configurazione per favorire la manutenzione.
Comporre URLs con localhost hardcoded nel default rappresenta un rischio di sicurezza se il file settings non è protetto. Valida sempre che le variabili d'ambiente siano configurate dove necessario.

findings.description: TRUST_REMOTE_CODE abilitato. Permette l'esecuzione di codice Python caricato dai modelli, creando una superficie di attacco significativa in entità di terze parti (es. Jina).
findings.recommendation: Impostare su False. Utilizzare solo modelli verificati o isolare l'ambiente di inferenza (sandbox).

findings.description: Endpoint HTTP non sicuri. Le configurazioni per TensorRT e vLLM usano 'http://', che espone il traffico a potenziali interception se non protetto su livello applicativo.
findings.recommendation: Usare 'https://' sempre possibile o connettere i servizi solo su loopback in ambienti strettamente contigui e sicuri.

findings.description: Endpoint HTTP non sicuri. Alta CONFIG probabilmente usata per produzione senza criptazione del canale.
findings.recommendation: Usare 'https://' sempre possibile o connettere i servizi solo su loopback in ambienti strettamente contigui e sicuri.

findings.description: Valori hardcoded. I parametri di configurazione sono codificati direttamente nel file, violando la best practice di non hardcoded parameters.
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare variabili d'ambiente o configurazioni esterne env.toml/env.json caricate all'avvio.

findings.description: Direzioni hardcoded. Definizione di percorsi nel filesystem localmente.
findings.recommendation: Configurare i percorsi di archiviazione tramite variabili d'ambiente o file di configurazione esterni.

findings.description: Model names hardcoded. Nomi dei modelli hardcoded potrebbero esporre dettagli di configurazione o logica interna dell'architettura.
findings.recommendation: Rimuovere i dettagli progettuali dal codice o rendere accessibili solo a livello di configurazione criptata.

findings.description: Logging imports should be at module level for performance, not inside exception handlers
findings.recommendation: Move 'import logging' to module level (around line 1) to avoid repeated imports

findings.description: Config function called at startup but no validation of existing config state
findings.recommendation: Consider caching the configs loaded at startup to avoid redundant re-initialization calls

findings.description: Hardcoded string patterns in list could benefit from configuration management instead of code
findings.recommendation: Move trace filtering patterns to settings/config file instead of hardcoded in code

error: llm: Nessun JSON valido nella risposta del modello

findings.description: Hardcoded project ID - sensitive identifier should not be in source code
findings.recommendation: Move to configuration file or environment variable (e.g., env variable or config.json)

findings.description: Multiple hardcoded parameters scattered in main function - violates Configuration Management best practice
findings.recommendation: Load configuration from external file or env variables at startup, not hardcoded in code

findings.description: Magic number threshold without documentation of why 0.7 was chosen
findings.recommendation: Add comment explaining the threshold choice or move to configurable constant

findings.description: No exception handling when calling async function - could crash if classify_documents_by_type raises exception
findings.recommendation: Wrap function call in try-except block with proper logging and graceful degradation

findings.description: Using print() instead of structured logging - future logging doesn't have domain/level parameters
findings.recommendation: Implement structured logging with domain and level parameters for better filtering capabilities

findings.description: Il file custom_settings.json può essere manipolato e letto da utenti con accesso al filesystem, potenzialmente esponendo dati sensibili o impostazioni critiche
findings.recommendation: Implementare cifratura dei dati sensibili e autenticazione controllata per l'accesso al file, oppure utilizzare un componente di configurazione gestito (come pydantic-settings con dotenv)

findings.description: Modelli LLM hardcoded nel codice di default (es. 'vllm/qwen3-30b-a3b-thinking-2507-awq-4bit') violano il principio di configurazione esterna
findings.recommendation: Spostare tutti i nomi dei modelli e le impostazioni sensibili in file di configurazione esterni (.env o custom_settings.json cifrato) con validazione dei valori

findings.description: URL hardcoded (localhost:8080) per OnlyOffice possono essere inseguri in produzione e non validati
findings.recommendation: Usare esclusivamente variabili d'ambiente per endpoint esterni, eliminare i fallback hardcoded nel codice

findings.description: I valori predefiniti di `os.getenv` nel codice possono essere sovrascritti insicure da `custom_settings.json` senza validazione
findings.recommendation: Implementare schema di validazione (es. pydantic) prima di sovrascrivere configurazioni Runtime, con whitelist di parametri modificabili

findings.description: Mancano validazioni per parametri ambientali numerici (INT_MAX, timeout, thread pool) che potrebbero essere impostati eccessivamente...
findings.recommendation: Implementare validazione con limiti ragionevoli per parametri di sicurezza e risorse (es. MAX_WAVES <= 5000, timeout > 0)

findings.description: I timeout per le connessioni HTTP non sono validati (0 potrebbe causare problemi
findings.recommendation: Implementare validazione con valori minimi obbligatori per timeout (es. CODE_EXECUTOR_TIMEOUT >= 60 secondi)

findings.description: I valori JSON sono caricati senza validazione dello schema, potenzialmente permettendo valori invalidi
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare una libreria tipo pydantic per validare la struttura json carica, rifiutando configurazioni sconosciute

findings.description: Il try-except generico nel caricamento di JSON mangia eccezioni senza logging dettagliato
findings.recommendation: Loggare l'eccezione specificamente con contesto (filename, custom_settings) e spawnare alert se config non è caricabile in produzione

findings.description: Il percorso di `custom_settings.json` è relativo dopo risoluzione, ma se il sistema non ha permessi di lettura sicuri il file potrebbe default-valore pericolosi
findings.recommendation: Implementare controllo di permessi sul filesystem e uso di mount point dédié per file di custom settings

findings.description: C'è un ambiente "debug" hardcoded che non può cambiare runtime per produzione
findings.recommendation: Implementare forzatura (hard requirement) di FLASK_ENV in produzione tramite misurazione dell'host o,se richiederebbe un git-tag production

findings.description: Input validation missing: trainig_objective parameter should be validated to prevent injection attacks
findings.code_snippet: if "prezzo" in self.training_objective.lower() or "sense of measure" in self.training_objective.lower():
findings.recommendation: Add input validation: trainig_objective = trainig_objective.strip()[:500] to limit length and prevent command injection

findings.description: Hardcoded file paths and extensions: 'datasets', '.structured.md', '.structured.json' should be externalized
findings.recommendation: Move to config file or use environment variables: dataset_path = os.path.join(BASE_DIR, EXTERNAL_DATASET_PATH)

findings.description: Hardcoded configuration values: base_quantity=100, price ranges 50-5000, multipliers 0.5/0.3/0.2 should be externalized
findings.recommendation: Remove defaults and load from config file at startup: base_quantity = config.get('base_examples')

findings.description: Multiple regex compilations in loops: re.search/re.findall called in _extract_price_examples loop (line 154-195) leading to repeated compilation
findings.recommendation: Compile regex patterns once at module level or method start: OBJECT_REGEX = re.compile(...)

findings.description: Code smells: Long function (synthesize_data spans lines 197-285) with 60+ lines, violation of single responsibility
findings.code_snippet: def synthesize_data(self, target_quantity: int, analysis_results: dict) -> List[dict]:
findings.recommendation: Break into smaller methods: _generate_price, _build_instruction, _apply_template

findings.description: Magic numbers without names: 500, 5000, 50, 0.3, 0.5, 0.8, 1.2 lack business meaning and should be named constants
findings.recommendation: Create constants: MIN_PRICE=50, MAX_PRICE=5000, PRICE_VARIATION=(-0.3, 0.5), THEN ITEM_COUNT=(3, 5)

findings.description: No directory existence check before file read/write operations
findings.code_snippet: all_files = [f for f in os.listdir(dataset_path) if os.path.isfile(os.path.join(dataset_path, f))]
findings.recommendation: Add directory validation: if not os.path.isdir(dataset_path): raise FileNotFoundError(...)

findings.description: Exception handling silent swallowing: Generic Exception caught without specific types
findings.recommendation: Use specific exception types: except FileNotFoundError as e: or except json.JSONDecodeError as e:

findings.description: No docstring for functions/params: format_output has minimal documentation
findings.recommendation: Add detailed docstring including Args, Returns, Raises sections

findings.description: No graceful degradation when documents fail to load - missing backup or default dataset
findings.recommendation: Implement graceful degradation: use sample data or log warning instead of raising exception everywhere

findings.description: Missing architecture documentation in /architecture/ folder for synthesizer system
findings.recommendation: Create /architecture/synthesizers.md documenting data flow, dependencies and integration points

findings.description: String replacement with regex in loop: repeated modifications to string output_content without optimization
findings.recommendation: Build output incrementally or pre-process template once instead of in each iteration

findings.description: Project ID hardcoded in code instead of configuration file
findings.recommendation: Move sensitive IDs to environment variables or configuration file (e.g., .env) as per Security Best Practice 5
findings.reference: Security Best Practices - Hardcoded credentials

findings.description: Type ID hardcoded in code instead of configuration file
findings.recommendation: Move sensitive IDs to environment variables or configuration file (e.g., .env)
findings.reference: Security Best Practices - Hardcoded credentials

findings.description: Similarity threshold hardcoded in code instead of being configurable
findings.recommendation: Extract configuration values to environment variables or settings file for better maintainability
findings.reference: Configuration Management - NO hardcoded parameters in code

findings.description: Debug information printed in stdout may be accessible by unauthorized users
findings.recommendation: Review debug logging and consider using structured logging with proper access controls (logging module with domain and level parameters)
findings.reference: Error Handling - Log errors with sufficient context for debugging

findings.description: No authentication/authorization checks before executing classification operation
findings.recommendation: Implement authentication and authorization checks before executing privileged operations
findings.reference: Security Best Practices - Implement proper authentication and authorization checks

findings.description: UUID constants scattered in code instead of referenced from configuration
findings.recommendation: Reference UUIDs from a configuration source or consult designated documentation file in architecture/ folder
findings.reference: Documentation - All features must be documented in a file inside the architecture/ folder

findings.description: Magic number 0.7 used without explanation
findings.recommendation: Define this as a named constant with documentation explaining its purpose and acceptable range

findings.description: Configurazioni hardcoded all'interno del file. Violazione di Best Practice #2: parametri costanti dovrebbero essere in file di configurazione esterni (.env o settings).
findings.recommendation: Estrai le liste di filtri in un file `config/settings.py` o da variabili d'ambiente.

findings.description: Manca l'uso di hint di tipo (Type Hints). Violazione di Best Practice #6 Code Quality.
findings.recommendation: Aggiungere hint di tipo per input e return (tipo None).

findings.description: Import di `logging` all'interno della funzione invece che all'avvio del modulo. Sconsigliato per modularità.
findings.recommendation: Spostare l'import di `logging` in alto del file insieme agli altri imports.

findings.description: Cattura troppo generica `Exception`, nasconde errori specifici. Violazione di Best Practice #4 Error Handling.
findings.recommendation: Specificare tipi di errori o ri-enunciare con log di debug più dettagliati.

findings.description: Gestione silenziosa dell'`ImportError` tramite `pass`. Potrebbe indicare un errore di configurazione critico non segnalato.
findings.recommendation: Transpose il warning al logging globale o segnare il fallimento come stato affidabile.

findings.description: L'uso di sottomansioni di stringhe con regex (re.sub) all'interno del loop principale di sintesi riduce drasticamente le performance per grandi quantità di dati. Le stringhe sono immutabili, generando nuova memoria ad ogni iterazione.
findings.code_snippet:
output_content = re.sub(
    r"(OGGETTO:|Oggetto:).*?($|\n)", 
    rf"\1 {new_object}\2", 
    output_content,
    flags=re.IGNORECASE | re.MULTILINE
)
findings.recommendation: Ristruttura il template in monolithic placeholders (es. '{OBJECT_NAME}') sostituisci I cicli con un passaggio di preparazione avanzato del template una volta, poi sostituisci segnaposto. Usa json heredoc o template string con backtick se possibile.

findings.description: Sottomansioni di stringhe multiple in un loop (Linee 135-155) causano O(N*M) complessità temporale dove N è il target quantity e M è la dimensione del template. Ottimizzazione minima per grandi volumi.
findings.code_snippet:
output_content = re.sub(
    r"(PREZZO TOTALE:|TOTALE:|Totale:)(\s*€\s*)[0-9.,]+(\s*€?)",
    rf"\1\2{new_price:.2f}\3",
    output_content,
    flags=re.IGNORECASE | re.MULTILINE
)
findings.recommendation: Usa un approccio Batch: recupera il template grezzo una volta, usa FASTPARSER o Saxon con SQLite se il dataset è masseic, o compilati le regexp fuori dal loop.

findings.description: Even though this is file I/O, reading full file content to memory (f.read()) and parsing regex over content is O(FileSize). For large structured markdowns, this consumes memory.
findings.code_snippet:
# Carica il contenuto strutturato
with open(file_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    content = f.read()
docs_data.append({"path": file_path, "content": content})
findings.recommendation: Usa generatori o streaming parser per evitare di caricare interi file di testo in memoria se il file è molto grande (>10MB). Utilizz libreria per parsing markdown (es. markdown-it-py) invece di regex grezza.

findings.description: I pattern Regex non sono pre-compilati. Questo forza alla creazione di nuovo oggetto ogni volta che la funzione viene chiamata, aumentando l'overhead.
findings.code_snippet:
# Cerca l'oggetto del preventivo (OGGETTO: ...)
object_match = re.search(r"(?:OGGETTO|Oggetto):\s*(.*?)(?:\n|$)", doc["content"], re.IGNORECASE)
findings.recommendation: Crea pattern compilati (`re.compile`) all'inizio delle rispettive funzioni e passali come argomenti.

findings.description: Calcolo di quantità basato su operazioni semplici ma hardcoded (0.5, 0.3, 1.0). Facilita debugging e consistenza se通过这些 parametri di configurazione esterna (es. config file o env).
findings.code_snippet:
if analysis_results.get("price_focus", False):
            multiplier += 0.5  # Aumenta se c'è focus sui prezzi
findings.recommendation: Sposta i moltiplicatori in un file di configurazione JSON o variabili d'ambiente, sostituendo i valori hardcoded nell'esecuzione produttiva.

findings.description: Scrittura di un file JSONL usando json.dumps() + write() nel ciclo di loop. Ogni iterazione apre/sforna RAM per l'object serializzazione.
findings.recommendation: Aggiorna serialization with buffering (e.g., json.dump in chunks) o usa chunked writer se il dataset è grande. json.dump con lines to avoid recreation of wrapper objects.

findings.description: Parameteri hardcoded nel codice (project_id, type_id, threshold) violano la best practice di Configuration Management e Code Agnosticism. Rende lo script inflessibile e non riutilizzabile per altri progetti o task.
findings.recommendation: Utilizza un file di configurazione (es. settings/.env o settings.yaml) e carica i parametri all'avvio. Usa variabili d'ambiente per valori procedurali.
findings.reference: Best Practices #1 (Code Agnosticism) & #2 (Configuration Management)

findings.description: Manca un wrapper di gestione eccezioni per l'invocazione asincrona. Se classify_documents_by_type fallisce con un blocco infinito o crash, non viene gestito logicamente o segnalato adeguatamente.
findings.recommendation: Avvolgi la chiamata asincrona in un blocco try/except. Implementa un mechanism di fallback o logging strutturato per errori di DB/external API.
findings.reference: Best Practices #4 (Error Handling) & #5 (System Prompts)

findings.description: Il debug script chiama l'operazione di classificazione senza evidenze di batching o caching. Se chiamato ripetutamente, soffre di ridondanza computazionale.
findings.recommendation: Implementa un layer di caching a livello di script se le chiamate sono costose e ripetitive (es. Redis o memoria locale), per evitare query multiple per lo stesso stato.
findings.reference: Anti-Patterns #3 (Performance) - Missing caching for expensive operations

findings.description: L'output è limitatamente a print standard. Non soddisfa il requisito di best practice per i log (dominio e livello per filtraggio automatico in futuro).
findings.recommendation: Utilizza un logger configurato con domini e livelli. Salvare gli eventi in un file structurato abitabile per l'analisi debug.
findings.reference: Best Practices #4 (Error Handling) - Log errors with sufficient context

findings.description: L'eccezione interna ("{e}") viene loggata direttamente, potenzialmente esponendo dettagli errati o stack trace interni agli accessori ai log di sistema.
findings.code_snippet: logging.warning(f"⚠️ TRACE FILTERS: Errore nella configurazione filtri globali: {e}")
findings.recommendation: Non interpolare l'oggetto exception direttamente. Usare un messaggio generico o loggare l'errore solo se necessario per il debugging senza esporre stack trace completi in produzione.

findings.description: Parametri hardcoded (project_id, type_id) violano la Best Practice #2 (Configuration Management). I progetti non dovrebbero essere identificati nel codice.
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare variabili d'ambiente o un file di configurazione JSON/XML per i project_id e type_id.

findings.description: Soglia di similarità hardcoded (0.7) viola Best Practice #6 (Code Quality - Magnetic Numbers) e #2 (Configuration Management).
findings.recommendation: Mappare il threshold in un file di configurazione o variabile d'ambiente operativo.

findings.description: Assenza di gestone errori (try/except) nella chiamata asincrona, Best Practice #4.
findings.recommendation: Implementare blocchi try/except attorno alle chiamate asincrone per catturare eccezioni e gestire degradamento.

findings.description: Uso di f-strings per console (print) invece del modulo logging viola Best Practice #4 (Error Handling - Log with sufficient context).
findings.recommendation: Sostituire le chiamate print con una istanza di logger configurativa sui log file.

findings.description: Percorso directory hardcoded nel metodo analyze_objective_and_docs. Viola le best practice di Configuration Management.
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare un percorso configurabile tramite <project.id> o un parametro esterno invece di hardcodare "datasets".

findings.description: File di output hardcoded nel metodo format_output.
findings.recommendation: Passare il nome del file come parametro o configurarlo in JWT/Config invece di hardcodarlo.

findings.description: Lista di oggetti generici hardcoded nel metodo synthesize_data, che riduce l'agnosticità del codice riguardo al task specifico.
findings.code_snippet:
sample_objects = real_objects + [
    "Servizio di Consulenza IT",
    "Sviluppo Software su misura" ...]
findings.recommendation: Caricare oggetti di riempimento da una configurazione esterna o evitare di hardcodare domini specifici nel codice.

findings.description: Utilizzo di "magic numbers"per il calcolo di quantità nei metodi estimate_data_quantity e synthesize_data.
findings.recommendation: Definire moltiplicatori e valori di soglia in un file di configurazione.

findings.description: Gestione delle eccezioni troppo generica (Exception) invece di tipi specifici, non permette debugging accurato.
findings.code_snippet:
except Exception as e:
    self.logger.warning(f"Errore nel caricare {filename}: {e}")
findings.recommendation: Catacare specificamente FileExistsError, FileNotFoundError o ValorErro analogamente a quanto richiesto.

findings.description: Logica di pulizia stringa (sostituzione punti e virgole) duplicata nel metodo _extract_price_examples.
findings.recommendation: Creare una funzione helper per normalizzare le stringhe numeriche e usarla in entrambi i branch.

findings.description: Prezzi e limiti di variazione hardcoded (500, 5000, 0.3) che vincolano il validatore specialistico.
findings.recommendation: Mappare valori limite di prezzi tramite parametri in models.json o JSON di configurazione.

findings.description: Path radice hardcoded (root_dir) rappresentato come percorso Windows fisso. Dovrebbe essere gestito via variabili d'ambiente o file di configurazione.
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare variabili d'ambiente (os.environ) o file .env per configurare il percorso della radice invece di hardcodarlo.
findings.reference: Configuration Management, Best Practice 2

findings.description: Doppio prefisso 'r\' nel percorso (r"r:\\...). Questo è un errore di sintassi che potrebbe causare risoluzione incomoda del percorso.
findings.recommendation: Correggere la sintassi del percorso in r"\BF_personaltune_mcp_soloedit" o utilizzare il percorso assoluto normalizzato con pathlib.
findings.reference: Code Quality, Best Practice 6

findings.description: Nessuna validazione del percorso prima di eseguire os.walk(). L'utente o l'ambiente potrebbe fornire un percorso non convalidato.
findings.code_snippet: for dirpath, dirnames, filenames in os.walk(root_dir):
findings.recommendation: Implementare validazione del percorso (py_filepath, pathlib.Path) e controlli di permessi prima dell'uso.
findings.reference: Security Best Practices, Validazione Input

findings.description: L'eccezione viene catturata ma solo stampata in console senza logging strutturato o contesto aggiuntivo.
findings.code_snippet:
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Could not read {filepath}: {e}")
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare un logger strutturato con dominio e livello (fermo in Best Practice 4) per tracciare gli errori in modo gestibile.
findings.reference: Error Handling, Best Practice 4

findings.description: Async function not properly utilizing async context - multiple imports inside function being called synchronously
findings.code_snippet:
from utils.tools.tool_registry import execute_tool
    from utils.langgraph.nodes.intent_analysis import intent_analysis_node
findings.recommendation: Move all imports to module level for better organization and avoid repeated imports on each call

findings.description: No memory cleanup - imported modules may hold references and accumulate if run multiple times
findings.code_snippet:
async def test_execution():
    from utils.tools.tool_registry import execute_tool
findings.recommendation: Consider using importlib for better memory management or import once at module level if needed

findings.description: No result caching detected - same tools may be called repeatedly without optimization
findings.recommendation: Implement result caching for frequently called tools or requests to avoid redundant execution

findings.description: Exception handling prints to stdout instead of using proper logging framework
findings.recommendation: Use proper logging with structured data instead of stdout for better observability and filtering

findings.description: Multiple context managers not properly cleaned up - traceback.print_exc() keeps reference to traceback
findings.recommendation: Ensure traceback objects are properly cleaned up after logging to avoid memory accumulation

findings.description: Hardcoded test values in code (test_proj, test_user, test_run) should not be hardcoded - violates Configuration Management best practice #2
findings.recommendation: Move configuration to .env file or environment variables - use os.getenv() for environment-specific config

findings.description: Extensive inline comments mix documentation with code, making code harder to maintain - violates Code Quality best practice #6
findings.code_snippet:
from utils.tools import tool_registry
	        # Usually get_all_tools_dict triggers discovery
findings.recommendation: Move inline notes to docstrings or separate documentation file at startup load time

findings.description: Print statements used instead of proper logging - violates Error Handling best practice #4, no domain/level filtering capability
findings.recommendation: Use logger from logging module: logger.info(f"Result type: {type(result).__name__}") at appropriate level

findings.description: No docstrings for functions - violates Code Quality best practice #6, method names should be self-documenting
findings.recommendation: Add docstring: "Test execution for intent analysis. Executes tool reflection via execute_tool with test parameters."

findings.description: Function name 'test_execution' is generic and doesn't indicate specific purpose - violates Code Quality best practice #6
findings.recommendation: Rename to descriptive name like 'execute_intent_analysis_test' to indicate specific test purpose

findings.description: Path di lettura vulnerabile a traversal. L'uso di self.project.id direttamente in os.path.join non valida se il percorso contiene '..' o caratteri speciali che potrebbero uscire dalla directory的训练_intended.
findings.recommendation: Sanificare il percorso validando che si mantenga sotto la radice 'datasets'. Utilizzare 'pathlib.Path.resolve()' o verificare esplicitamente che il percorso non modifichi la directory padre.

findings.description: Validazione mancante della directory di output. L'uso di self.output_dir potrebbe essere manipolato se configurato impropriamente, portando esposizione a scrittura arbitraria di file.
findings.recommendation: Assicurarsi che self.output_dir sia una directory allocata specificamente per questo task e che i permessi di scrittura siano limitati.

findings.description: Hardcoded parameters. La directory 'datasets' è scritta direttamente nel codice richiudendo_hardcoding.
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare variabili d'ambiente o file di configurazione per la radice dei dati (es. DATA_DIR) invece di hardcode.

findings.description: Logging delle eccezioni. I dettagli delle eccezioni potrebbero contenere informazioni sensibili sul sistema o struttura dati.
findings.recommendation: Loggere solo l'errore tecnico generico senza stack trace completi o dati file path specifici se possibile.

findings.description: Path 'root_dir' is hardcoded with a specific Windows absolute path, making the script non-portable and failing the configuration management best practice.
findings.recommendation: Move 'root_dir' to an environment variable or configuration file, and validate paths dynamically at runtime.

findings.description: Generic 'except Exception' handler hides the specific error type, preventing precise debugging and logs less structured information.
findings.code_snippet:
except Exception as e:
                print(f"Could not read {filepath}: {e}")
findings.recommendation: Replace with 'try... except (FileNotFoundError, PermissionError) as e:' to handle specific file I/O issues and gather better context for debugging.

findings.description: Using 'errors=ignore' when reading source code can silently mask encoding errors, preventing detection of corrupted or unsafe files.
findings.code_snippet: with open(filepath, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:
findings.recommendation: Remove 'errors='ignore'-' and rely on default encoding behavior. If files have encoding issues, they should be skipped with a logged warning, not ignored.

findings.description: Missing shebang line ('#!/usr/bin/env python3'), preventing the script from running directly via system shell.
findings.recommendation: Add '#!/usr/bin/env python3' at the very top of the file to make it executable and compatible without absolute interpreter paths.

findings.description: Drochester hardcoding di `root_dir` che impedisce la portabilità e nasconde percorsi sensibili o interna
findings.recommendation: Utilizza una configurazione esterna o variabili d'ambiente per il path della root directory.
findings.reference: 2. Configuration Management

findings.description: Hardcoded string `target_string` viola il principio di configurabilità e separazione delle logiche
findings.recommendation: Mostrare questa stringa in un file di configurazione esterno o passare come parametro.
findings.reference: 2. Configuration Management, 6. Code Quality

findings.description: Uso di `errors="ignore"` nasconde anomalie di codifica nei file target e能讓 essere silenziosamente corrotti
findings.code_snippet: with open(filepath, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:
findings.recommendation: Gestire gli errori di encoding esplicitamente con try-except specifici o configurare l'encoding in base a ciò che ci si aspetta.
findings.reference: 7. File and Resource Management

findings.description: Utilizzo di `print()` per logging invece di un logging framework, rende difficile filtrare e aggregare i log
findings.recommendation: Implementare un sistema di logging standardizzato con dominio e livello.
findings.reference: 4. Error Handling

findings.description: Use of generic `except Exception`此次活动 catches-all exceptions without distinguishing errors, making debugging harder

findings.reference: 4. Error Handling

findings.description: Emoji used in log messages (🔍, ✅, 📊, 💬, 💾, ❌) which makes automated log parsing difficult
findings.recommendation: Remove emojis from log messages and use prefixes like [INFO], [WARNING], [ERROR] instead

findings.description: Hardcoded model name (vllm/qwen3-30b-a3b-thinking-2507-awq-4bit) should use environment variable
findings.recommendation: Move default configuration to dedicated configuration file or use environment variable only without hardcoded defaults

findings.description: Sync and Async implementations are nearly identical and should be refactored

findings.recommendation: Pass configuration as parameters instead of using global variables, makes code testable

findings.description: Tracing should use a proper decorator instead of wrapping inside the function
findings.recommendation: Create a proper decorator for wrapping functions with LangSmith tracing

findings.description: Client creation without explicit cleanup/teardown
findings.recommendation: Use context manager or explicit close() for Client resource management

findings.description: Repeated console messages in `if __name__ == "__main__"` block

findings.description: Inconsistent casing in internationalized logs (⚠️, 🔧, ✅, 🔍)

findings.description: Long and repetitive code in _run_and_eval_sync and _run_and_eval_async

findings.description: Function names like `eval_wrapper` and `auto_eval` lack clear purpose
Caricamento dell'intero file in memoria tramite f.read() può causare picchi di RAM su file grandi o taglierie.

findings.description: Creazione della lista 'lines' con splitlines() duplica l'adesione nella memoria dopo aver già caricato il contenuto.
findings.recommendation: Mantieni il file aperto e leggi le righe direttamente all'annuncio della stringa target per evitare список completo.

findings.description: The function 'os.walk' is used without parallelization, limiting concurrency on multi-syst.

findings.description: Hardcoded model name in defaults may leak infrastructure details

findings.description: Project name hardcoded could reveal internal service identification
findings.recommendation: Use a configurable project name from environment variables or config files

findings.description: User input and sensitive data logged without sanitization

findings.description: No validation on function arguments passed to wrapped functions

findings.description: Hardcoded model version 'v1' should be configurable

findings.description: Hardcoded project name 'personaltune-agents' used multiple times throughout the file

findings.description: Missing type hints on several functions also used as utilities (example_X functions)

findings.description: Internal project name 'personaltune-agents' is hardcoded and printed, potentially exposing internal project structure.

findings.description: Evaluate_single_run does not have explicit error handling for potential failures in LLM calls.

findings.description: Hardcoded default model path in configuration

findings.description: Using emojis in log messages affects automated processing

findings.description: Execution flow is split into multiple global config variables
findings.recommendation: Centralize configuration using a dedicated Config class loaded once at startup
findings.reference: Configuration Management - Best Practice #2

findings.description: asyncio.run() called synchronously may block event loop in async context
findings.recommendation: Use asyncio.run() cautiously; prefer integrating as part of application event loop
findings.reference: Anti-Patterns - #3 (Synchronous I/O operations)

findings.description: API client list_runs() called on every evaluation - causes unnecessary database/API calls with every function invocation
findings.recommendation: Cache the last run metadata and reuse it. Only fetch new runs when needed, not on every evaluation call.

findings.description: Evaluator instance created fresh every time (create_evaluator called at line 140)
findings.recommendation: Cache evaluator instances by model and reuse them. Create a global evaluator registry keyed by model_id.

findings.description: No async/await usage for the Client calls - list_runs() and create_feedback() are blocking operations in an async context
findings.recommendation: Use async LipsSharp client with await list_runs() instead of synchronous client. Implement proper async error handling.

findings.description: Client connection not properly managed - creates new Client() instance on each call, no connection pooling
findings.recommendation: Use a singleton Client instance or connection pool. Reuse connection across evaluations to reduce overhead.

findings.description: Code duplication between _run_and_eval_sync and _run_and_eval_async - logic repeated for both paths
findings.recommendation: Refactor to a single _run_and_eval function that checks iscoroutinefunction and awaits appropriately. Reduce code duplication.

findings.description: No exception handling for closing LangSmith evaluation objects - potential resource leaks if evaluator has finalizers
findings.recommendation: Implement proper cleanup with try/finally block to close evaluator resources. Consider context managers for the evaluator.

findings.description: API key hardcoded in code as 'sk-secret123'
findings.code_snippet: env={'api_key': 'sk-secret123'}  # Will be redacted in traces
findings.recommendation: Remove hardcoded credentials. Load from environment variables or secure config file

findings.description: Magic strings for internal types ('demo.chat', 'agent.calculator')
findings.recommendation: Define these as constants or configuration parameters

findings.description: Manual sys.path modification for local development
findings.recommendation: Use proper package configuration or entry point instead of path manipulation

findings.description: No graceful degradation if tracer.start_run fails
findings.recommendation: Add exception handling for tracer initialization and start_run call

findings.description: Try-except block raises exception at end without re-raising properly
findings.recommendation: Raise exception after logging to maintain error context in stack trace

findings.description: Long print statements with f-strings could be split for readability
findings.recommendation: Separate logging and user-facing output for cleaner separation of concerns

findings.description: Modifica di sys.path non è consigliata; può causare conflitti di import o caricare script locali in contesti di produzione.
findings.recommendation: Utilizza un installazione standard di dipendenze virtualizzate (venv/virtualenv) invece di manipolare path systemici. Rimuovi questa riga.
findings.reference: Best Practice 2 (Configuration Management), Best Practice 9 (Dependency Management)

findings.description: Livello di logging impostato su DEBUG è troppo verbose per ambienti non debug; può rallentare le performance e saturare i log.
findings.recommendation: Imposta il livello di log con variabili d'ambiente (es. LOG_LEVEL=INFO) e leggile al startup.
findings.reference: Best Practice 4 (Error Handling), Best Practice 7 (Log Management)

findings.description: Uso di parametri hardcoded (project_id, user_id, run_id) che dovrebbero essere configurati esternamente per essere riutilizzabili.
findings.recommendation: Passa questi parametri come argomento alla funzione o dai accessi a file di configurazione (settings.toml/.env).
findings.reference: Best Practice 2 (Configuration Management), Anti-Patterns: Magic numbers

findings.description: Uso di print() per tracciare errori invece di usare l'istanza logger configurata con contesto specifici.
findings.recommendation: Sostituisci con logger.exception(e) per includere la stack trace manse in modo corretto nel log.
findings.reference: Best Practice 4 (Error Handling)

findings.description: Presenza di eccessivi commenti sparsi nel codice che rompono il flusso logico senza aggiungere valore documentale.
findings.code_snippet: # allow internal default enabling of disable_mediation=False to kick in?
findings.recommendation: Riduci la quantità di commento o spostali in documentazione separata (GUIDELINES.md).
findings.reference: Best Practice 8 (Documentation), Anti-Patterns: Maintainability

findings.description: Import execution Lazy (all'interno della funzione) potrebbe creare overhead di avvio se non necessario, anche se qui accettabile per test.
findings.recommendation: Considera di mantenere gli import a livello di modulo per chiarezza, o mantieni se è una funzione temporanea.
findings.reference: Best Practice 6 (Code Quality)

findings.description: Hardcoded API URL and configuration values in code instead of external configuration
findings.recommendation: Move configuration to external config file or environment variables for better maintainability and environment support

findings.description: String concatenation in loop (full_content += content) creates O(n²) complexity
findings.recommendation: Use a list to collect chunks, then join at the end with 'join()' for O(n) complexity

findings.description: Using synchronous requests for streaming endpoint - should use async HTTP client like aiohttp for better performance
findings.recommendation: Use aiohttp or httpx with async support for streaming endpoints to improve concurrency and resource usage

findings.description: Hardcoded model ID 'gpt-3.5-turbo' makes code less flexible for different model configurations
findings.recommendation: Make model_id configurable via parameters or config file for model switching support

findings.description: Print statements called directly in streaming loop without proper async considerations
findings.recommendation: Consider using async logging or async print for better control in high-concurrency streaming scenarios

findings.description: No caching strategy for repeated requests to same endpoints
findings.recommendation: Implement caching layer with TTL for expensive API calls when content requires regeneration

findings.description: Hardcoded API URL without environment variable configuration
findings.recommendation: Move API_URL to environment variable via environment variables or external config file

findings.description: Hardcoded user credentials in payload (user_id and project_id)
findings.recommendation: Use environment variables for user identification and configuration from config files

findings.description: No HTTP response body cleanup after streaming loop completes
findings.recommendation: Ensure proper cleanup of response connection by calling response.close() after the loop

findings.description: Hardcoded model identifier in request payload
findings.recommendation: Extract model ID from configuration to allow switching models without code changes

findings.description: Using print() instead of proper logging framework
findings.recommendation: Implement proper logging with domain and level for filtering purposes

findings.description: Using plain HTTP instead of HTTPS for API communication
findings.recommendation: Use HTTPS for all external communications to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks

findings.description: No input validation on user message content
findings.code_snippet: "message": "Write a short story about a robot learning to paint.",
findings.recommendation: Add input validation and sanitization for all user-provided inputs to prevent injection attacks

findings.description: No retry mechanism for failed HTTP requests
findings.recommendation: Implement retry logic with exponential backoff for transient network failures

findings.description: No proper handle closing for response connection
findings.recommendation: Use context manager or ensure response is properly closed after use with try-finally

findings.description: Magic number used for SSE data format offset
findings.recommendation: Define HTTP_STATUS_LINE_LENGTH as a constant for better readability and maintainability

findings.description: API connection uses HTTP (unencrypted) instead of HTTPS, exposing all data in transit including user messages, conversation IDs, and tracking information to potential interception
findings.recommendation: Use HTTPS for all production API connections: "https://localhost:5000/api/chat_v2" or configure the API server to support TLS encryption

findings.description: Sensitive conversational data (messages, conversation_id, tracking_info, duration) is printed directly to console, potentially exposing user data to anyone with access to the terminal
findings.recommandation: Avoid logging sensitive user content to console. Log only necessary metadata (request IDs, timestamps, status) and never expose user messages in plain text

findings.description: User identifiers (user_id: "demo-user") and project_id ("demo-project") are hardcoded, which assumes specific authentication context and could bypass proper authorization checks
findings.recommendation: Use environment variables or runtime context for identifiers. Authenticate users through proper authentication mechanism before making requests

findings.description: API URL, model_id, and user context are all hardcoded in the code. These should be configurable through environment variables or configuration files
findings.recommendation: Use environment variables for API_URL, model_id, and context values. Example: API_URL = os.getenv("API_URL", "http://localhost:5000/api/chat_v2")

findings.description: No validation of payload content before sending. User input, message length, or model compatibility could cause unexpected behavior or resource exhaustion
findings.code_snippet: "message": "Write a short story about a robot learning to paint.",
findings.recommendation: Add input validation for message content (length, character types). Use libraries like validators or pydantic to ensure data conforms to expected schema

findings.description: No timeout configuration for requests. Potential for hung connections on slow/unresponsive servers, wasting resources and blocking execution
findings.recommendation: Add timeout parameter to request calls: timeout=30. For streaming, add lifespan.Handler for connection cleanup

findings.description: Content-Type hardcoded as "application/json" instead of using a constant or configuration, making changes difficult and error-prone
findings.recommendation: Define a constant for content type: CONTENT_TYPE = "application/json" and use it in the request

findings.description: Print statements use emojis and lack structured logging. No log domain/level differentiation per audit best practices
findings.recommendation: Use a proper logging library (logging or structlog) with domain and level parameters. Log to files, not print() statements

findings.description: API key 'sk-secret123' is hardcoded directly in the code, exposing sensitive credentials risk.
findings.recommendation: Store credentials in .env file and load them securely at runtime. Never commit secrets to code repositories.
findings.reference: Security Best Practices #5 - Sensitive values should be in .env file

findings.description: Exception details printed to console (line 95) may expose internal system information to users/intruders during runtime.
findings.recommendation: Log errors internally without exposing stack traces or sensitive details. Use structured logging with appropriate log levels.
findings.reference: Error Handling #4 - Log errors with sufficient context for debugging

findings.description: Internal application data (LLM response, tool results) is printed directly to console, potentially leaking sensitive information.
findings.recommendation: Avoid printing internal data to console. Use dedicated logging module for internal diagnostics.
findings.reference: Security Best Practices #4 - Log errors with sufficient context for debugging

findings.description: Hardcoded model 'gpt-4' and user_id 'demo-user' reduce configuration flexibility and obfuscate intent.
findings.recommendation: Use configuration parameters for model names and user IDs to improve flexibility and maintainability.
findings.reference: Code Quality #6 - Avoid code duplication (DRY principle)

findings.description: Utilizzo di identificatori di modello hardcodati (qwen2.5-14b-instruct, deepseek-chat). Violazione del principio di Agnosticità del Codice (Best Practice #1).
findings.recommendation: Spostare i nomi dei modelli in una configurazione esterna o variabile d'ambiente per garantire che il codice sia etero-funzionale.

findings.description: Identificazione del progetto hardcodata (personaltune-agents). Violazione del principio di Gestione Configurazione (Best Practice #2).
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare variabili d'ambiente (es. PROJECT_NAME) o un file di configurazione per evitare dipendenze da nomi specifici nel codice.

findings.description: Uso di print() per il logging invece di strumenti di log strutturati. Mismatch con Best Practice #4 (Logging e Debug).
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare le funzioni `logger.info` o `logger.debug per journalizzare le informazioni, rimportando il contesto di configurazione logging.

findings.description: Nomenclatura funzionale 'example_X_' potrebbe scoraggiare l'uso in produzione senza modifiche strutturali.
findings.recommendation: Considerare la separazione dei pattern di esempio in un sotto-modulo di test per mantenere il file principale pulito e professionale.

findings.description: API_URL, model_id e contesto utente sono hardcoded. Violazione Best Practice #1 e #2.
findings.recommendation: Sposta la configurazione in un file settings esterno o usa file .env.

findings.description: Model ID "gpt-3.5-turbo" è hardcoded, violando Best Practice #1 (Llama Agnostic).
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare parametri di configurazione inseribili alla runtime.

findings.description: Utilizzo di protocollo HTTP (non HTTPS) per la comunicazione esterna, violando Best Practice #5.
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare HTTPS per tutte le comunicazioni esterne o verificare che localhost sia sicuro.

findings.description: Eccezione json.JSONDecodeError viene swallowing silently con "pass".
findings.recommendation: Loggare l'errore anziché ignorarlo per tracciamento e debug (Best Practice #4).

findings.description: Struttura JSON del payload e logica di connessione duplicati tra le due funzioni.
findings.recommendation: Rifattorizzare in una classe Comune o funzione generica per ridurre la duplicazione (Best Practice #7).

findings.description: Split di dato hardcoded e stringhe magiche per visualizzazione (es. separator 60).
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare costanti per separatori o configurazioni di layout.

findings.description: Hardcoded secret value 'secret123' in environment configuration.
findings.recommendation: Remove hardcoded credentials. Use a .env file or secure environment variables (os.environ) and load them externally before passing to the tracing system.

findings.description: Broad exception catching ('Exception') swallows errors without proper logging context.
findings.recommendation: Use specific exception types and ensure all exceptions are logged with domain/level parameters before handling, adhering to error handling best practices.

findings.description: Manual sys.path manipulation relies on specific directory structure and breaks package isolation.
findings.recommendation: Ensure the package is installed and importable, or refactor to not rely on dynamic path insertion which tightens coupling to the script's location.

findings.description: Hardcoded CLI commands create stale references in console output.
findings.recommendation: Replace hardcoded CLI commands with references to documentation or configuration objects to ensure accuracy when tools evolve.

findings.description: Manual modification of sys.path can shadow installed modules and compromise import isolation.
findings.recommendation: Remove manual sys.path manipulation. Rely on standard virtual environments (venv/).
findings.reference: Configuration Management 2, Dependency Management 9

findings.description: Logging level set to DEBUG exposes excessive noise and implementation details that shouldn't be accessible in public environments.
findings.recommendation: Use INFO or WARN levels for security-sensitive applications. Log DEBUG only for specific debugging tools.
findings.reference: Logging 4, Code Quality 6

findings.description: Exception handling prints full exception objects and stack traces, potentially leaking internal state or stack walker info.
findings.recommendation: Do not print stack traces to stdout. Use structured logging with specific log levels.
findings.reference: Error Handling 4

findings.description: Test data for user_query and context is hardcoded, which may not reflect real-world input validation scenarios if this script is mistakenly used in production logic.
findings.recommendation: Use input validation fixtures or environment variables for test data instead of hardcoded strings.
findings.reference: Code Quality 6, Input Validation 5

findings.description: Codice sincrono per operazioni asincrone. Le chiamate LLM e i database sono I/O bound. L'assenza di async/await riduce il throughput e aumenta i tempi di attesa se usato in contesti concorrenti.
findings.code_snippet:
emit_llm_call_start(
    tracer,
    model="gpt-4",
    prompt="Calculate 2+2 and explain the result",
    params={"temperature": 0.7, "max_tokens": 100},
    purpose="reasoning"
)
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare metodi asincroni (es. await tracer.assemble_trace) se il provider di tracing supporta I/O async per migliorare la concorrenza.

findings.description: Risultati calcolati vengono generati da zero ogni esecuzione. In scenari di produzione con richieste ricorrenti dello stesso contenuto, non c'è caching del risultato o della chiamata LLM.
findings.recommendation: Implementare meccanismi di memoization o cache per evitare chiamate ridondanti a LLM per query identiche o utileto.

findings.description: Implicite现货 calls speed up. Le operazioni di tracing implicite (via tracer) potrebbero bloccare il thread se non gestite correttamente.
findings.code_snippet:
tracer.start_run(
    entrypoint="demo.chat",
    args={"query": "What is 2+2?", "user_id": "demo-user"},
    env={"api_key": "sk-secret123"}  # Will be redacted in traces
    )
findings.recommendation: Ottimizzare gli accessi al database durante il tracing, o spostare il tracing su un layer async per non bloccare I/O di altri thread.

findings.description: Use of os.system() for pip install and setup.py is inefficient and unpredictable; proper package management should be used
findings.code_snippet:
os.system('pip install gfpgan')
os.system('python setup.py develop')
findings.recommendation: Use replica's package management or cfg_files like requirements.txt with proper dependency installation

findings.description: Using os.system('wget') for downloading models can consume unpredictable amounts of RAM and CPU; no progress tracking
findings.recommendation: Use requests library or faster download with progress tracking, handle connection timeouts

findings.description: Model weights are not cached or validated; no size limits enforced on temporary outputs
findings.code_snippet:
if tile <= 100 or tile is None:
    tile = 0
findings.recommendation: Implement model caching with metadata validation, add output size constraints

findings.description: All operations are synchronous; no async/await consideration for I/O operations
findings.recommendation: Convert setup and predict to async operations for better resource handling

findings.description: No caching between predictions; each prediction re-downloads or re-processes models independently
findings.recommendation: Implement model/chunk caching with TTL to avoid redundant processing

findings.description: No explicit release of model weights or optimizer references between calls; memory could accumulate
findings.recommendation: Release self.upsampler and self.face_enhancer references after use with del()

findings.description: Output path uses tempfile.mkdtemp() each time without reuse opportunity for multiple tiles
findings.recommendation: Consider reusing temp directory with cleanup instead of creating new temp each call

findings.description: Sensitive API key hardcoded in environment parameters and explicitly marked as a secret in comment, but no encryption or secure storage is used.
findings.recommendation: Remove hardcoded credentials. Load environment variables using os.environ or a dedicated secrets management library like python-decouple or python-dotenv from .env file.

findings.description: Full exception traceback and sensitive error info are printed to stdout via print statements, potentially exposing internal stack traces or debug information to unprivileged users.
findings.recommendation: Use a structured logging module (e.g., logging.exception) instead of print for errors. Sanitize output before exposing it to end-users, and never depend on string formatting exceptions directly to user-facing output without context and filtering.

findings.description: sys.path is manually modified using string manipulation, which can introduce security risks or ambiguity in application dependency loading.
findings.recommendation: Use package structure and dependencies defined in requirements files or proper module imports. Avoid arbitrary directory insertion into sys.path.

findings.description: Uso di os.system per installare pacchetti a runtime (pip install). Questo comportamento è insicuro e non adatto per deploy containerizzati immutabili.
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare un file requirements.txt e installare durante l'constructor del container o arrestare il container. L'ambiente dovrebbe essere pronto all'uso o installazione nelle fase di build.

findings.description: Uso di os.system per eseguire comandi shell. È vulnerabile ad attacco di iniezione e dovrebbe essere evitato.
findings.recommendation: Rimuovere completamente questa riga. L'installazione delle librerie dovrebbe avvenire tramiteDependency Menagment (requirements.txt).

findings.description: Uso di os.system per scaricare pesi del modello. Questa logica blocca l'avvio del servizio se non c'è connessione internet e lascia il sistema instabile.
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare un meccanismo di cache o un容器 layer specifico per i pesi. Inviare una richiesta HTTP diretta tramite una libreria Python (requests) in un thread non bloccante o usare un docker layer pre-buildato con i weights.

findings.description: A derivazione hardcodata dei path dei file e dei link URL. Non è configurabile tramite variabili d'ambiente.
findings.recommendation: Mettere i path in una configurazione esterna (es. settings.json o variabili d'ambiente) per permettere flessibilità.

findings.description: Uso di print per la registrazione degli errori invece di un logger strutturato. Le informazioni di contesto sono scarse.
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare il modulo 'logging' con appropriate impostazioni di dominio e livello. Inserire tutti i dati necessari per il debug.

findings.description: Uso di un generico except Exception con silent swallow. Oscura errori importanti senza gestire l'anomalia.
findings.recommendation: Logare l'errore con trace info e se possibile gestire l'eccezione specificamente. Non lanciare funzioni in produzione con solo 'print'.

findings.description: Uso di numeri magici '300', '100', '2' senza spiegazioni. Complica la manutenzione.
findings.recommendation: Definire costanti al livello modulare con nomi descrittivi (es. MINIMAL_HEIGHT_FOR_ENHANCEMENT = 300).

findings.description: Presenza di codice commentato che non obiettivo. Suggestivo rimuovere codice non funzionali.
findings.recommendation: Rimuovere le righe commentate per pulire il codice e ridurre il rumore degli scanner.

findings.description: Variabili non esplicative. 'img_mode', 'h', 'w' usano variabili non molto significative.
findings.recommendation: Rinominare i metodi onde abbiano descrizione più chiara, ad es 'original_shape' o 'output_mode'.

findings.description: os.system() called without validation for pip install - arbitrary code execution risk
findings.recommendation: Use proper package installation methods with try-except blocks and validate package names before installation
findings.reference: Security Best Practices - Hardcoded credentials & Configuration Management

findings.description: os.system() with wget downloads weights from external URLs without verification - potential malware injection
findings.recommendation: Use proper package managers or verify file checksums (SHA256) before downloading
findings.reference: Security Best Practices - Secure Direct Object References

findings.description: Multiple os.system() calls for downloading model weights expose deployment to network security risks
findings.recommendation: Download modeulos during CI/CD pipeline with checksums verified, not at runtime
findings.reference: Security Best Practices - Configuration Management

findings.description: No exception handling for wget/HTTP requests - deployment can fail gracefully
findings.recommendation: Wrap download operations in try-except blocks with proper logging
findings.reference: Error Handling - Implement graceful degradation when dependencies fail

findings.description: Hardcoded analysis URLs for all weights - makes deployment inflexible and creates security dependency
findings.recommendation: Move URLs to config file or .env, with option to override during deployment
findings.reference: Configuration Management - NO hardcoded parameters in code

findings.description: Print statements used for logging instead of proper exception handling with context
findings.recommendation: Implement proper logging framework with domain and level parameters as mentioned in best practices
findings.reference: Error Handling - Log errors with sufficient context for debugging

findings.description: RuntimeError exception and global exception handler prints instead of handling gracefully
findings.recommendation: Implement proper error logging and graceful degradation, return informative error to user
findings.reference: Error Handling - Never silently swallow exceptions

findings.description: Commented out code remaining in production code - dead code pollution
findings.recommendation: Remove commented lines or convert to TODO items for future reference
findings.reference: Anti-Patterns - Dead code (unused functions/variables)

findings.description: os.listdir() and file operations without proper directory existence check in clean_folder
findings.recommendation: Add os.path.isdir(folder) check before iterating
findings.reference: File and Resource Management - Handle file system errors gracefully

findings.description: Magic numbers (300, 100, 400, 200) used without constant definition
findings.recommendation: Define as constants at module level with descriptive names (e.g., MIN_IMAGE_HEIGHT)
findings.reference: Anti-Patterns - Magic numbers (unexplained constants)

findings.description: Heavy DNS requests during setup that may timeout or cause resource exhaustion
findings.recommendation: Add HTTP timeouts to downloads, implement retry logic, consider async downloads
findings.reference: Performance Anti-Patterns - Synchronous I/O operations

findings.description: Hardcoded tile values (100) without configuration option
findings.code_snippet: if tile <= 100 or tile is None:
findings.recommendation: Make threshold a configurable parameter
findings.reference: Configuration Management - NO hardcoded parameters in code

findings.description: Missing documentation file in architecture/ folder for this predictor
findings.recommendation: Create architecture/RealESRGAN_README.md documenting API endpoints and parameters
findings.reference: Documentation - All features must be documented in a file inside the architecture/ folder

findings.description: Use of os.system() for installing packages and downloading files is a code smell. While parameters are static here, it's a direct violation of security best practices and makes maintenance difficult.
findings.recommendation: Use pip directly with subprocess.run() or pip install directly. Avoid os.system() entirely.
findings.reference: Security Best Practices - 5. Security Best Practices

findings.description: os.system() calls should not be used in production code. Use proper subprocess with sudo if needed.
findings.recommendation: Use pip properly or proper dependency installation methods, not os.system().
findings.reference: Security Best Practices - 5. Security Best Practices

findings.description: Dependencies are installed dynamically during setup without proper validation. This could install malicious packages if paths are controlled.
findings.recommendation: Validate file integrity (SHA256), check content before execution, use proper download libraries with verification.
findings.reference: Security Best Practices - 5. Security Best Practices

findings.description: No authentication/authorization is shown in this code layer. Hardcoded model versions and paths.
findings.recommendation: Implement proper dependency management with checksums and proper access control.
findings.reference: Security Best Practices - 5. Security Best Practices

findings.description: Generic except Exception as error catches all exceptions and prints them to stdout, potentially leaking information.
findings.code_snippet:
except Exception as error:
            print('global exception: ', error)
findings.recommendation: Implement proper logging with context tracing, don't print exceptions to stdout, implement specific exception handling.
findings.reference: Audit Best Practices - 4. Error Handling - Use proper exception handling with specific exception types

findings.description: RuntimeError exception stops control flow but prints generic message, no proper recovery or context.
findings.recommendation: Implement graceful degradation, log with context, return proper error codes to the caller.
findings.reference: Audit Best Practices - 4. Error Handling - Implement graceful degradation when dependencies fail

findings.description: Temporary folder handling has no proper cleanup guarantee. Clean_folder function has exception but outside proper scope.
findings.recommendation: Ensure proper resource cleanup happens finally, verify cleanup with success/failure tracking.
findings.reference: Audit Best Practices - 7. File and Resource Management - Close file handles properly - Clean up temporary files - Handle file system errors gracefully
